latest.title: TotalEnergies - Angola: Start-up of the New Gas Consortium Quiluma Offshore Gas Field
latest.content:
17 Mar 2026 08:26
TotalEnergies (Paris:TTE) (LSE:TTE) (NYSE:TTE) announces the start of production from the Quiluma field, in which the Company holds an 11.8% interest alongside its partners Azule Energy (37.4%, operator), Cabinda Gulf Oil Company (31%) and Sonangol E&P (19.8%).
This is the first development of a non-associated gas field in Angola and the gas produced will be a stable and important source of gas supply for the Angola LNG plant that is delivering LNG to both the European and Asian markets. At plateau, the project will produce around 330 million cubic feet per day of gas, equivalent to approximately 2 million tons of LNG per year.
â€œBy ensuring a sustainable long term gas supply for the Angola LNG plant, the project strengthens Angolaâ€™s ability to supply LNG to international markets in the long run, including Europe and Asia,â€ stated
Mike Sangster, Senior Vice-President Africa, Exploration & Production at TotalEnergies.
About TotalEnergies in Angola
TotalEnergies has been present in Angola since 1953 and today employs around 1,500 people across different business segments. With a diversified portfolio, deep offshore operated assets representing more than 45% of the countryâ€™s oil production, service stations in partnership with Sonangol and renewable energy projects, TotalEnergies in Angola is a key player in supporting the countryâ€™s sustainable energy transition.
About TotalEnergies
TotalEnergies is a global integrated energy company that produces and markets energies: oil and biofuels, natural gas, biogas and low-carbon hydrogen, renewables and electricity. Our more than 100,000 employees are committed to provide as many people as possible with energy that is more reliable, more affordable and more sustainable. Active in about 120 countries, TotalEnergies places sustainability at the heart of its strategy, its projects and its operations.

latest.title: Impact of Middle East Conflict on TotalEnergies Activities
latest.content:
16 Mar 2026 07:00
TotalEnergies (Paris:TTE) (LSE:TTE) (NYSE:TTE):
Confirmation of information released on Companyâ€™s website on March 10, 2026
Production has been shut down or is in the process of shutting down in Qatar, Iraq and UAE offshore, representing approximately 15% of our total output. Onshore UAE production (~210 kb/d TotalEnergies share) is not affected by the conflict at this stage. The Middle East barrelsâ€™ CFFO is lower than our portfolio average due to higher taxation, and these 15% of our volumes account for ~10% of Upstream cash flow. Growth of our accretive barrels is expected to come overwhelmingly from outside the Middle East in 2026, meaning that a higher oil price more than offsets the loss of Middle East production: an $8/b increase in the Brent price is enough to offset the expected 2026 CFFO from our Iraq, UAE offshore and Qatar assets at $60/b. Operations at the Satorp refinery are continuing normally for now and are supplying the Saudi domestic market. The impact of LNG production shutdowns in Qatar on our LNG trading activities is limited (around 2 Mt expected in 2026), as most Qatari LNG is marketed by QE.
TotalEnergies is continuing to monitor the evolution of the situation on the ground and will update you in case of material change of the above.
Cautionary Note
The terms â€œTotalEnergiesâ€, â€œTotalEnergies companyâ€ or â€œCompanyâ€ in this document are used to designate TotalEnergies SE and the consolidated entities that are directly or indirectly controlled by TotalEnergies SE. company_30s.what_they_do: TotalEnergies is a French multinational that explores, produces, and sells oil and natural gas while steadily building a renewable portfolio that includes solar, wind, and hydrogen.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company operates one of the world’s largest pipeline networks and has a global footprint that gives it a competitive edge in securing energy supplies. Its transition to renewables positions it to benefit from the long‑term shift away from fossil fuels, while its existing oil and gas assets keep it profitable in the short term.
company_30s.current_state: TotalEnergies is a mature, cash‑generating enterprise that is now investing heavily in renewables. It is shedding non‑core oil assets and securing long‑term power contracts. The share price sits at a low point relative to its historical valuation, reflecting a recent sell‑off that has pushed the stock into a deep discount.

rns_narrative: The company is busy signing long‑term renewable power deals – a 1‑GW solar project for Google’s Texas data centers, a 800‑GWh supply contract for a European paper mill, and a partnership with Tikehau Capital for public charging networks.
At the same time it is trimming its oil exposure – divesting interests in Africa, the Middle East and certain LNG projects – to reduce debt and focus on higher‑margin renewables.
Corporate governance changes, such as the transaction in own shares and the stabilisation notice, signal a management intent to tighten balance sheet discipline.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is silent but a handful of posts praise the new renewable contracts.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few analysts point to the Google solar deal and the European renewable supply agreements as catalysts that could lift the stock toward its mid‑range.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders worry that the oil‑price decline and regulatory delays could keep the share languishing, especially if the company’s debt load remains high.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from indifferent to cautiously hopeful as the renewable contracts surface, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there is no public hype, so a news event could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Feb 11 2026 earnings release – any upside surprise will be a clear trigger.
Announcement of the 1‑GW solar contract with Google (Feb 9) – a high‑profile deal that signals a shift to renewables.
A new renewable supply agreement with Airbus or a European utility – would cement the company’s transition narrative.
Possible divestment of a non‑core oil asset – could improve the debt profile and boost confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike (≥3× average daily) coupled with a price break above the recent 20‑day moving average. A positive earnings surprise or a new contract announcement would confirm the bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score suggests tight compression – the stock is still in the early phase of a recovery cycle. The deep crash indicates the market has already capitulated, so a mean‑reversion play is plausible if the above catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits 70‑95 % below its 52‑week high, giving a huge upside window. If the price recovers to the middle of its range, the return could approach 200‑250 %. Historical RED signals show that deep crashes often double or triple the entry price after a catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Oil‑price volatility and the company’s sizeable debt could keep the share depressed. If the renewable contracts fail to materialise or the earnings miss expectations, the stock may stay near its current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200 % (if it hits mid‑range). Potential downside: limited to the current price (the crash has already happened). The asymmetry is highly favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the recent high with volume at least 3× average. Use a 20 % stop below the entry. Allocate 10 % of the portfolio to this position and scale in if the trend continues.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the earnings release or a new renewable contract is announced. If the price stays below the 20‑day moving average for 10 consecutive trading days, reassess the thesis.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal threat would be a catastrophic oil‑price collapse that erodes the company’s core assets.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TotalEnergies’ share sits in a deep crash; the February renewable contracts and earnings release create a clear catalyst window, and the upside potential far outweighs the limited downside – a classic contrarian long.
